title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (July 8)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington homes sold for $750,000 and $525,000, built in 1973 and 1952 respectively, with three bedrooms each.
Acton's highest sale was a Colonial built in 2009, sold for $1,325,000, featuring four bedrooms and three baths.
The most expensive home in Arlington was built in 1909, sold for $2,230,000, with three bedrooms and three baths.

title: Foxborough files counterclaim to Patriots, Kraft Group’s lawsuit over annual stadium licensing fees
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Foxborough filed a counterclaim against Kraft Sports and New England Patriots over stadium's financial obligations.
The town argues the $1 million annual fees are public safety reimbursements, not unlawful licensing fees.
Foxborough seeks dismissal of the lawsuit and claims plaintiffs violated the lease's implied good faith agreement.

title: UMass Lowell soccer player killed in Ohio car crash
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: UMass Lowell soccer player Tobias Forsythe, 21, died in a car crash in Ohio on July 5.
Forsythe's car was rear-ended by a semi truck; driver Asrarov Bekhzod is charged with evidence tampering.
Forsythe had transferred to UMass Lowell for the spring 2026 season, majoring in economics.

title: Co-owner of Maine lumber mill dies weeks after fire, explosion that killed 2 others
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden J. Robbins, co-owner of Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, died from injuries sustained in a May 15 explosion.
The explosion and fire at Robbins Lumber have resulted in three deaths, including a firefighter and an assistant fire chief.
The explosion, determined accidental, injured 12 others and is being investigated by the Maine Department of Public Safety.

title: Plymouth DA seeks assault and battery charge against Brockton mayor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth DA's office seeks assault and battery charge against Brockton Mayor Moises Rodrigues.
A clerk-magistrate hearing will determine if there's enough evidence for charges against Rodrigues.
Accusations stem from an alleged incident with a 17-year-old student at a Brockton parade.

title: Police searching for tandem moped riders after shots fired in Roxbury
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Police seek public help locating two suspects from a moped tandem ride involved in Roxbury shots-fired incident.
The incident occurred on June 30 on Schuyler Street, with suspects seen heading towards Elm Hill Avenue.
Photos released show suspects wearing black masks; any information should be reported to detectives at (617) 343-4275.

title: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan whose reaction to trade went viral
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown comforted six-year-old fan Giovanni Jean, whose emotional reaction to Brown's trade to the 76ers went viral.
Giovanni met Brown at his MIT program and expressed support, handing him a heartfelt letter.
Brown reassured the young fan they would remain friends, fulfilling a promise made via his Twitch stream.

title: Fatal Cambridge shooting renews criticism of council vote to end ShotSpotter contract
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was shot and killed in Cambridge on July 4; police were alerted by a passerby an hour later.
The shooting has intensified debate over Cambridge's decision to end its ShotSpotter contract, impacting gunshot detection response times.
Cambridge officials plan a community meeting to discuss the incident, while Bautista's family seeks justice through a GoFundMe campaign.

title: Jaylen Brown on Celtics trade: Boston is home and no organization can take that away
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers, maintaining his dedication to Boston community work.
Brown's 7uice Foundation launched its fifth annual summer bridge program at M.I.T. in Cambridge, focusing on S.T.E.A.M subjects and leadership.
Massachusetts declared July 7th as 7uice Foundation Day, honoring Brown's contributions during his 10 seasons with the Celtics.

title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, of Lawrence, arrested for drunk driving after crashing a Porsche on I-95 in Woburn.
Tarshi's vehicle, a 2017 Porsche 911 with a New Hampshire veteran plate, was traveling wrong-way before crash.
Potential charges include possession of a forged New Hampshire RMV document found in the Porsche's glove box.
